It records Creative and Adventure, both castle routes, the CaxeMap/CaxeFlow authoring path, child-friendly visual rules, en/es-MX, original art, portability boundaries, compiler-quality flywheel, release gates, and the separate Ivvy companion owner haxe_c-xge.22. The document is explicit that the game is not playable yet.

title: [HXC-SCAF] Replace legacy untyped seams with typed boundary adapters
description: Audit the pre-existing Reflect, Dynamic, Any, untyped, open-record, and unchecked-cast seams in repository-owned Haxe. Replace avoidable uses with closed typedefs, enums, typed exceptions, generics, or narrow boundary adapters. Genuinely unavoidable preview compiler, vendored Reflaxe, or JSON decoding boundaries must be isolated, documented, validated immediately, and prevented from propagating into semantic compiler logic. This discovery must not broaden E2.T04.
acceptance_criteria: Repository-owned Haxe has an explicit searchable inventory and narrowly justified allowlist for remaining untyped constructs. Avoidable Dynamic, Reflect, Any, untyped, and unchecked casts are removed from compiler semantic paths. Remaining external-boundary uses terminate in closed typed models with negative tests. A governance guard rejects new unapproved uses. Bootstrap, typed-AST, diagnostics, snapshots, compiler-server, and exhaustive all-source gates remain green.
notes: Final audit: 154 repository-owned Haxe files are governed by docs/specs/typed-boundaries.json. Compiler semantic paths contain no Dynamic, Any, Reflect, untyped, DynamicAccess, unchecked cast, private-access metadata, or raw __c__ use. The three exact retained occurrences are a validated Json.parse ownership adapter returning ReflaxeOwnershipMetadata, a diagnostic negative-test cast, and an IRCoverage fixture parameter representing the real Haxe Dynamic language type. Local npm test and pre-commit passed, including compiler-server isolation, 14 snapshot suites, native smoke, and 56 governance tests. GitHub Governance run 29496015578 attempt 2 passed required GCC, required Clang, pinned toolchain, and license/provenance for commit 949069a.
close_reason: Completed discovered compiler-hygiene task haxe_c-6h2.1 in commit 949069a. The audit now covers all 154 repository-owned Haxe files with a source-exact machine inventory, strict schema, Haxe-aware scanner, mutation tests, pre-commit wiring, and Governance enforcement. Compiler semantic paths no longer use Dynamic, Any, Reflect, untyped, DynamicAccess, unchecked casts, private-access metadata, or raw __c__. Closed diagnostic, typed-AST, declaration-plan, symbol-failure, target-platform, and Reflaxe callback models replace the avoidable carriers. Exactly three reviewed occurrences remain: Json.parse is confined to ReflaxeOutputWriter.decodeOwnershipMetadata and immediately validated into ReflaxeOwnershipMetadata with malformed/wrong-shape tests; one cast forges an impossible diagnostic ID only in a rejecting negative test; one Dynamic parameter is test source representing the real Haxe language semantic and maps explicitly to IRTDynamic. Evidence passed: npm test; bash scripts/hooks/pre-commit; npm run test:typed-boundaries; bootstrap, typed-AST/compiler-server, diagnostics, all-source, project-emitter, HxcIR, semantic and native suites; 14 registered snapshots; 56 governance tests; jq and syntax checks; git diff --check; bd dep cycles --json returned []; bd lint --json returned zero issues. GitHub Governance run 29496015578 attempt 2 passed license/provenance, pinned toolchain, required GCC, and required Clang for SHA 949069a; attempt 1 had only a transient nm 10-second timeout and the identical required GCC lane passed on retry. Compatibility: this is compiler/governance hygiene only, with no generated-C, profile, runtime-policy, environment, hxrt selection, stdlib support, or public-ABI behavior change. User-language Dynamic and reflection lowering remain explicitly deferred to planned E4/E5 semantic work.

title: [HXC-DISC] Ratify generated-output and runtime redistribution license treatment
description:
## Decision needed

Decide and document the licensing treatment for generated C sources and headers, selectively emitted hxrt source/binaries, compiler-provided templates, generated manifests, and user-authored inputs. Keep source publication separate from package/release claims and obtain owner/legal review before promising an exception or permissive runtime grant.

## Rationale

The repository GPL-3.0-only license governs repository-owned source but does not, by itself, settle whether every compiler output is a derivative work or what terms should accompany copied runtime/template material. Users need a deliberate, consistent answer before release packaging, and the answer affects emitted SPDX data, notices, templates, and runtime selection.

## Alternatives Considered

- Assume all generated artifacts inherit GPL-3.0-only. Rejected as an unsupported blanket inference that could unnecessarily constrain users.
- Promise permissive or exception-based treatment immediately. Rejected because the exact runtime/template boundaries and owner-approved terms have not been reviewed.
- Leave the question undocumented until release. Rejected because implementation could harden conflicting output and packaging behavior.
- Track an explicit pre-release decision with runtime-free and runtime-linked fixtures. Selected as the fail-closed path.
acceptance_criteria: An accepted decision names the license/SPDX treatment for generated C and headers, generated manifests, bundled hxrt source and binaries, templates, and copied third-party inputs; public docs explain user obligations without implying legal advice; release packaging emits the decided notices and SPDX/provenance metadata; representative runtime-free and runtime-linked package fixtures pass the license policy validator.

title: [HXC-DISC] Bootstrap hxc on Eval and dogfood a native C artifact
description:
Requirement IDs: HXC-CLI-002, HXC-CLI-003, HXC-CLI-010
PRD sections: §18, §25 M8

Context: The owner ratified an Eval-first bootstrap for the optional hxc ergonomics layer, followed by dogfooding the same CLI core through reflaxe.c into a native C-built executable.

Scope: Build one target-neutral Haxe CLI core; expose it initially through haxe --run / haxelib run on Eval; add narrow host-service seams; compile the same core through reflaxe.c once required sys/runtime semantics are supported; keep direct Haxe/HXML plus a native C compiler sufficient to bootstrap releases.

Non-scope: Reimplementing the Haxe compiler, requiring native hxc to build itself, maintaining divergent Eval/native command implementations, or choosing Neko/HashLink as an additional mandatory runtime.

Validation: Run identical CLI fixtures under Eval and the generated native executable; compare exit codes, stdout/stderr, JSON, normalized build plans, and failure behavior; prove clean bootstrap without a preinstalled hxc.

Compatibility: Eval remains the development/recovery implementation and differential oracle. Native hxc is a packaged dogfood artifact, not the authoritative compiler frontend.

Documentation: README, PRD §18, installation/release documentation, and E8 packaging evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- haxe --run / haxelib run executes the shared CLI core on Eval with no extra host runtime.
- Direct Haxe/HXML plus the selected C compiler can build native hxc without an existing native hxc.
- Eval and native fixtures agree on parsing, configuration, plans, diagnostics, JSON, stdout/stderr, and exit status.
- Native packaging uses generated C from reflaxe.c and records runtime features/toolchain inputs.
- Eval remains documented and tested as a bootstrap/recovery path; no circular self-host dependency exists.

title: [HXC-E4-T11] Version runtime ABI and feature manifests
description:
Stable plan key: E4.T11
Requirement IDs: HXC-RT-004, HXC-RT-010, HXC-RT-012
PRD sections: §13.1, §16.8

Context: Define internal/public runtime ABI compatibility, feature schema versions, and generated-code checks.

Scope: Define internal/public runtime ABI compatibility, feature schema versions, and generated-code checks.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E4.T01, E4.T02

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated code rejects incompatible runtime major versions.
- Feature manifests are machine-diffable.
- Public exported APIs do not accidentally expose unstable runtime structs.
- Release artifacts include runtime source/build provenance.
close_reason: Completed E4.T11. Generated runtime-using program.h now emits a structural C11 _Static_assert for HXC_RUNTIME_ABI_MAJOR; test/string_output/run.py proves a same-major minor change links and runs, while major 1 fails compilation with the expected message and leaves no executable. runtime/hxrt/features.json and docs/specs/runtime-features.schema.json are deterministic schema 2 records for internal ABI 0.4.0, compatibility policy, every source SHA-256, the sorted source-set digest, and strict C11/C++17 build baselines. RuntimeFeaturePackager rechecks source bytes before creating GeneratedFile values, with an HXC9000 tamper fixture. The catalog runner discovers every public hxc struct into the forbidden application-export inventory; generated hxc.abi.json remains analyzed-no-public-exports with empty exports/types. runtime_smoke.c and public_header_cpp.cpp prove the versioned runtime headers under strict C11 and C++17. Portable and metal share this contract; runtime-free output still emits no hxrt metadata or ABI assertion; generated public application ABI and supported release artifacts remain intentionally unsupported for E7/E10. Verification: npm test; npm run test:runtime-features; npm run test:string-output; npm run test:all-sources; npm run test:typed-boundaries; npm run snapshots:check; protected pre-commit gates. Local gcc identifies as Clang, so required real GCC remains the post-push Governance CI gate.

title: [HXC-E7-T03] Emit public C headers and implementation wrappers
description:
Stable plan key: E7.T03
Requirement IDs: HXC-ABI-001, HXC-ABI-002, HXC-ABI-003
PRD sections: §16.3, §16.4

Context: Generate standalone, namespaced, documented C headers and wrappers that translate to internal calling conventions.

Scope: Generate standalone, namespaced, documented C headers and wrappers that translate to internal calling conventions.

acceptance_criteria:
- Header compiles as C11 and C++17 with extern-C guards.
- Include guards, visibility, calling conventions, and integer types are portable.
- No private/internal runtime symbol leaks.
- C and C++ consumers need only installed public artifacts.
notes: Use the ratified @:c.export/@:c.name/@:c.header vocabulary and extend the central typed contract allowlist rather than creating export-local annotations. E7 owns ownership/error/version metadata admission, ABI-safe type checks, stable headers/wrappers, native assertions, and independent C/C++ consumers; the M0 snapshot is not a stable ABI. Header authoring follows a Haxe-first ladder: ordinary Haxe exports produce the normal public header; typed c.* declarations and validated metadata express precise ABI intent; a narrow typed declarator/header DSL may be admitted only for demonstrated C grammar gaps such as function pointers, bitfields, flexible arrays, anonymous unions, calling conventions, or selected preprocessor contracts; raw header text remains an audited last resort. Users do not manually author guards, dependency ordering, or repeated prototypes. Keep portable Haxe APIs separate from explicit metal adapters, and make generated headers human-readable without sacrificing a stable ABI manifest.

title: [HXC-E3-T02] Lower Haxe enums as tagged unions
description:
Stable plan key: E3.T02
Requirement IDs: HXC-SEM-010, HXC-SEM-016, HXC-SEM-024, HXC-MAC-002
PRD sections: §12.6

Context: Emit readable discriminants and payload unions, including recursive and generic cases.

Scope: Emit readable discriminants and payload unions, including recursive and generic cases.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E3.T01

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Nullary constructors may optimize to enums when ABI-safe.
- Payload access is tag-checked in safe/debug configurations.
- Pattern matches are exhaustive and compile to structured switch code.
- Recursive representation avoids illegal infinitely sized C types.
close_reason: Completed E3.T02 in commit 2c113eff85b974a44c0bf64ccb73e045b8ba68c7. Concrete fieldless Haxe enums now lower to private native C enums when payload-free; payload enums lower to an explicit discriminant, per-constructor payload structs, a payload union, and an outer value struct. Concrete generic instances such as Option<Int> and Option<Bool> receive deterministic enum-local representations. Recursive payload edges are finite typed pointers to stable function-local backing, while recursive parameters and returns fail closed with source-positioned HXC1001 until escape and lifetime ownership exists. HxcIR schema 4 now preserves named payloads, explicit discriminants, enum construction, checked payload projection, tag matching, and structured tag switches; validation rejects direct payload representation, non-exhaustive or redundant-default switches, and infinite direct layouts. Emission remains structural CAST with CSymbolRegistry ownership, checked projections occur after tag control flow, and the runtime plan records the runtime-free bounded-haxe-enum-values decision. Verified locally with npm run test:enum-lowering, npm run test:hxc-ir, npm run test:capabilities, npm run test:typed-boundaries, npm run format:haxe:check, and npm test. Reviewable evidence is in docs/enum-lowering.md, test/enum_lowering/expected, test/hxc_ir/expected, and the generated capability manifest/README block. GitHub Governance run https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29592343866 passed the pinned toolchain, required GCC and Clang native enum lanes, both build-adapter matrices, formatting, provenance, and history scanning. General monomorphization remains owned by E3.T03; reference and aggregate payloads, escaping recursive values, identity/equality/reflection, and public ABI exposure are intentionally not claimed by this bead.

close_reason: Completed E0.T09 for HXC-COMP-014, HXC-QA-001, and HXC-QA-007. Added the schema-backed eight-lane fixture contract in docs/specs/fixture-taxonomy.json, fixture-taxonomy.schema.json, fixture-case.schema.json, docs/testing.md, and canonical test/{positive,negative,ast,snapshot,runtime,differential,abi,performance}/ directories; mapped every current suite to an argument-array runner, declared assertions, status, and expected evidence without promoting direct AST/IR or independent native seeds into generated-Haxe claims. Added scripts/test/snapshots.py with explicit check and targeted/all update modes, two-render determinism, semantic JSON/exact structural diffs printed before per-file atomic writes, catalog-root/format ownership, stale-file detection, CI update refusal, and owning-suite validation; scripts/ci/check_fixture_policy.py, governance unit tests, npm/CI/pre-commit wiring enforce registry parity, complete expected-file ownership, and schema-valid explicit example assertions. The absent documented examples mismatch is scoped separately as discovered issue haxe_c-od2.7. close_reason: Completed E3.T03 in ae501f0. Closed reachable generic static functions and admitted enum values now use versioned length-prefixed full semantic keys, SHA-256 compact IDs with full-key collision verification, request-local bounded worklists that terminate direct, mutual, and non-stationary recursion, and shared structural strict-C11 instances. Successful specialized builds emit schema-validated hxc.specializations.json with sorted reachability reasons, recursion membership, exact per-function bytes and digests, conservative enum-layout attribution, payload totals, and a 524288-byte hard budget; no-specialization builds omit and stale-delete the sidecar. Dynamic, open, function-count, type-count, and code-size overflow paths fail at exact source positions with HXC1001 and no plausible output. Evidence: test/generic_specialization fixtures and expected/hxc.specializations.json; npm test; npm run test:generic-specialization; npm run snapshots:check; scripts/hooks/pre-commit; npm run public:preflight; Governance https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29607814869, including required GCC and Clang strict C11 O0/O2 closed-generic jobs plus pinned-toolchain and GCC/Clang build adapters. Compatibility: portable and metal profiles and auto/minimal/none runtime policies produce the same runtime-free specialization payload and empty hxrt selection; hosted affects only the existing executable entry boundary; no public generic ABI is introduced, and generic classes, containers, Dynamic boxing, and exported generic contracts remain with their owning beads. The pinned Haxe source confirms typed pack+name is the nominal identity: public same-package duplicate names are rejected and private secondary types carry a hidden module segment, so no target-owned module suffix is added.

description:

close_reason:
Requirements HXC-SCAF-001, HXC-SCAF-004, and HXC-DOC-007 are satisfied in implementation commit 32bc18e. docs/specs/bootstrap-inventory.json and its strict schema now expose 37 stable current-checkout capability boundaries as implemented, scaffold-only, experimental, or unsupported, with exact dispositions, real evidence paths, stable Beads owners, compatibility limits, and seven explicit false product claim gates. The manifest reports 18 implemented bounded slices, 5 scaffold-only seeds/contracts, 1 opt-in experiment, and 13 unsupported or unvalidated surfaces.

The README What exists today block is generated mechanically from that manifest. hxc doctor is not fabricated: its manifest contract remains explicitly unsupported and absent, and E8.T06 is required to consume the same capability IDs when it implements human and JSON output. AGENTS and PRD Section 32 define the status meanings and prohibit promotion from adjacent evidence. Stale aspirational claims about present examples, Run.hx, CLI schema/templates, bindgen tools, export files, and a generated requirements inventory were corrected.

scripts/ci/check_capability_manifest.py isolates JSON decoding at a validated object boundary and rejects unknown shape, schema/checker drift, invalid status/disposition or area/authority pairs, unstable ordering, unsafe/missing evidence paths, unknown Beads owners, README drift, accidental CLI appearance, and premature stdlib/production/performance/release claims. Twelve mutation tests cover the positive repository contract and negative cases. package scripts, pre-commit, CI policy, and the Governance workflow all run the guard.

Local evidence passed: npm run test:capabilities; 12 focused capability governance tests; npm run test:governance with 45 total governance tests; npm test across the pinned toolchain, all owned Haxe sources, compiler suites, 14 snapshots, and native smoke; jq empty docs/specs/*.json and package metadata; Python byte compilation; bd dep cycles returned []; bd lint returned zero findings; whitespace/diff checks; and the full staged pre-commit chain including Gitleaks and strict Apple Clang 17 C/C++ execution. Local gcc was correctly skipped because it identifies as Clang. Remote Governance run https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29477942291 passed pinned-toolchain, license/provenance plus the new capability check, Ubuntu GCC, and Ubuntu Clang.

Compatibility: governance and documentation only. Portable/metal semantics, runtime policy, environments, generated C, hxrt selection, and public ABI are unchanged. The manifest explicitly selects no runtime feature. General Haxe lowering, full stdlib, hxc/doctor, examples, bindgen/export, production readiness, performance, supported platform/release tuples, and stable public ABI remain deferred to their named owner beads. The requested typed todo TUI was recorded separately as haxe_c-w5w.1 under E9, blocked by the real CLI, repository, and bindgen prerequisites rather than being hidden in this task.

close_reason: Completed HXC-SCAF-004, HXC-COMP-014, and HXC-CLI-010 in implementation commit 7640096. Added the typed CDiagnosticId registry and CDiagnostic emission boundary; all target-owned Haxe call sites now use registered IDs instead of raw prefixes, with profile and canonical remediation in human compiler output. docs/specs/diagnostics.json schema 2 defines 12 current diagnostics, exhaustive HXC0000-HXC9999 ranges, allocation/retirement/wording policy, allowed severities, phases, kinds, required source spans, profile policy, summaries, and remediation. Existing bootstrap, typed-C, typed-AST, all-source, declaration-plan, and HxcIR tests now assert stable IDs plus essential fields where appropriate; actual compiler probes verify profile, remediation, and Haxe source anchoring. Documentation, fixture taxonomy, npm, pre-commit, toolchain, CI, README, architecture, PRD, and AGENTS are synchronized. Evidence passed: npm test; npm run test:toolchain; npm run test:diagnostics; three repeated typed-AST runs; all 20 governance unit tests; jq empty docs/specs/*.json and package metadata; Python byte compilation; bd dep cycles --json []; bd lint --json zero; whitespace and diff checks; staged local-path and Gitleaks guards; full tracked pre-commit. Preserve the freestanding no-libc-allocation compile path and add C/C++ ABI/layout evidence when hardening.
close_reason: Completed HXC-SEM-019, HXC-RT-005, and HXC-RT-011 in implementation commit dce97ac. The provisional allocator now has checked size arithmetic; explicit power-of-two alignment; canonical zero-size behavior; failure-atomic allocate/resize out-parameters; allocator-identity comparison; and an owned hxc_allocation record whose allocator follows move, resize, and disposal across boundaries. Hosted over-alignment is portable and freestanding builds require an explicit allocator. Executable evidence covers overflow, invalid and over-alignment, zero-size, OOM/reallocation preservation, callback misalignment, cross-boundary ownership, and exact release identity in runtime/hxrt/test/allocator_contract.c and runtime_smoke.c. scripts/ci/runtime_smoke.py proves the freestanding allocator object has no unresolved libc allocation symbols, runs a static-arena custom allocator, runs Clang AddressSanitizer plus UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer, and links C layout facts into a C++17 public-header consumer. The governed ABI case is test/abi/allocator-contract/case.json and the full contract is docs/allocator-abi.md. Verification passed with npm test, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, npm run snapshots:check, npm run test:runtime-features, npm run test:fixture-policy, npm run test:capabilities, npm run test:typed-boundaries, npm run format:haxe:check, python3 scripts/ci/check_ci_policy.py, and git diff --check. The runtime remains native-seed-only: generated Haxe selection stays unchanged and runtime-free, and E4.T11 still owns stable public ABI/version compatibility.

Added the native-seed-only dependency-closed array feature over alloc, with contiguous unboxed primitive/reference storage, checked deterministic capacity growth, failure-atomic allocation, exact-slot alias handling across relocation and insertion shifts, lifecycle copy/assign/destroy callbacks, rollback, reverse destruction, bounds access, resize, removal, and owner move. close_reason: Completed in commit 339eaee. The compiler now lowers bounded direct nonescaping constructor use through typed HxcIR schema 6 into strict C11 aggregate storage, preserving Haxe super/field/body order, language default field values, and evaluation of arguments to proven-trivial constructors. Constructor failure is explicit status control flow with deterministic partial-initialization cleanup; portable and metal builds remain runtime-free. Constructor cycles, native layouts, generic classes without bounded specialization, and escaping/conditional/aliasing uses fail closed with source-positioned HXC1001 and no plausible artifact. Evidence includes canonical positive, negative, HxcIR, runtime-plan, determinism, Eval-oracle, strict GCC/Clang O0/O2, and C++17 generated-header consumer fixtures. Verified with npm test, npm run public:preflight, staged pre-commit and pre-push gates, and Governance run https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29623095057 (all jobs successful). Deferred intentionally: general allocation and escaping objects to E4, instance/virtual dispatch to E3.T06, interfaces to E3.T07, general exceptions to E4.T09, and public ABI ownership/export policy.

title: [HXC-E4-T03] Implement string runtime and encoding contract
description:
Stable plan key: E4.T03
Requirement IDs: HXC-PROD-005, HXC-SEM-006, HXC-RT-008
PRD sections: §12.3, §13, ADR 0004

Context: Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.

Scope: Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E4.T02, E0.T01

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Embedded NUL and invalid encoding policy are tested.
- Borrowed CString lifetime and owned conversion are explicit.
- Common literal/concat/compare paths are direct and allocation-aware.
- String feature can link without object/GC/reflection when possible.
notes:
ADR 0004 is accepted and normative. Implement private immutable valid UTF-8 storage; every Haxe index is a Unicode-scalar index; no implicit normalization; malformed UTF-8 lossy decode replaces each maximal ill-formed subsequence with U+FFFD and checked decode returns an explicit result. Bytes stays binary. Safe CString conversion rejects embedded NUL and distinguishes source-lifetime borrow from allocator-owned conversion. String must remain independently linkable from object/GC/reflection. The E0 Eval probe observed astral length/code 1/128512, composed/decomposed lengths 1/2, and embedded-NUL length 3.
Terminology clarification: ADR 0004's exact lossy policy is one U+FFFD per maximal subpart of an ill-formed UTF-8 byte sequence, not one replacement for an arbitrarily long malformed run.
Production String work is blocked by target-carrier decision haxe_c-od2.6. Haxe 4.3.7 Cross selects target.utf16/utf16 before initialization macros and cannot be relabeled safely; do not implement scalar UTF-8 semantics behind a compiler configuration that types UTF-16 branches. Reuse the structural String/StringTools/UnicodeString snapshot from haxe_c-od2.4 once the carrier is resolved.
Carrier decision haxe_c-od2.6 is resolved: the exact Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 CustomTarget(c) production snapshot selects scalar String/StringTools/UnicodeString branches with target.unicode and without target.utf16/utf16. Hosted/freestanding capabilities are target-owned; Eval remains only an independent oracle. String implementation may proceed when its planned dependencies are ready.
close_reason: Implemented E4.T03 as a bounded native UTF-8 scalar string contract: immutable valid UTF-8 values, scalar length/access/slice, checked decoding and deterministic maximal-subpart lossy decoding, direct literal/compare/hash paths, allocation-aware concat and mutable buffering, allocator identity, and explicit borrowed versus owned CString lifetimes with embedded-NUL rejection. The feature graph remains NativeSeedOnly with the exact string -> alloc -> status -> runtime-abi closure and links without object, GC, reflection, or dynamic symbol families. Generated-Haxe String lowering, full stdlib parity, and a stable public ABI remain deferred to their owning beads. Evidence: npm test; npm run test:string-runtime; npm run test:runtime-features; strict GCC 16 and Clang C11 O0/O2; ASan/UBSan; C++17 header consumer; clang static analysis; snapshot, capability, typed-boundary, governance, and exhaustive all-source gates.

title: [HXC-E3-T06] Implement virtual method dispatch and override validation
description:
Stable plan key: E3.T06
Requirement IDs: HXC-SEM-013, HXC-SEM-014, HXC-SEM-015
PRD sections: §12.7

Context: Emit minimal vtables only for reachable polymorphic calls and preserve override signatures.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

acceptance_criteria:
- Nonvirtual/final/direct calls remain direct.
- Vtable slots are deterministic across modules.
- Override variance/representation mismatches are rejected.
- Dispatch reports explain each indirect call.
close_reason:
Implemented in commit 598b92a. Reachable ordinary overridable class calls now lower through explicit schema-7 HxcIR dispatch layouts and minimal strict-C11 root vtables, while final/private/inline/@:nonVirtual/exact-final-receiver and super calls remain direct. Slots and finalized names are deterministic; concrete construction binds the selected table; typed self thunks adapt derived receivers; and full semantic argument/return representation mismatches fail at exact source spans with HXC1001 before output. The conditional schema-1 hxc.dispatch.json report explains every reachable direct/indirect choice, layout, slot, table, implementation, adapter, and empty runtime-feature set.

Evidence: docs/virtual-dispatch.md; docs/specs/dispatch-report.schema.json; src/reflaxe/c/lowering/CBodyDispatch.hx; CDispatchReport.hx; structural HxcIR/validator/emitter changes; positive, negative, native, snapshot, reversed-order, locale, portable/metal, runtime-none, and warm compiler-server fixtures under test/virtual_dispatch. npm run test:toolchain passed the complete toolchain matrix and all 26 registered snapshot suites; npm run test:native passed strict Apple Clang C11/C++17, O0/O2, ASan/UBSan, hosted/freestanding, and independent-header gates; npm run test:governance passed policy, capability, typed-boundary, fixture, license, and 57 governance tests; the exact staged pre-commit hook repeated the compiler/native/snapshot/governance gates successfully. Local optional GCC lanes reported the expected identity skip because the available gcc commands resolve to Clang; required GCC and Clang lanes remain enforced by Governance CI.

Compatibility: portable and metal use the same representation, no hxrt feature is selected, and no public/stable C object ABI is exposed. Interface dispatch, dynamic methods, closures, generic methods/classes, escaping or heap objects, reflection/type tests, exception-bearing virtual methods, and public exported class ABI remain fail-closed for their named follow-up tasks.

Implementation commit 09f0d66 was pushed and Governance run 29475304617 passed pinned-toolchain, license-and-provenance, strict GCC, and strict Clang jobs: https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.c/actions/runs/29475304617. title: [HXC-E3-T01] Lower anonymous structures and value structs
description:
Stable plan key: E3.T01
Requirement IDs: HXC-SEM-009, HXC-SEM-014, HXC-MAC-002
PRD sections: §12.5, §17.2

Context: Represent structurally known records as named/deduplicated C structs with clear value/reference behavior.

Scope: Represent structurally known records as named/deduplicated C structs with clear value/reference behavior.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E2.T11, E1.T03

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- Field order/layout policy is deterministic.
- Anonymous structural equality does not accidentally become C nominal incompatibility.
- By-value copies and address-taking are explicit.
- Metal @:c.layout(c.Layout.Struct) validation rejects unsupported layout combinations.
notes:
The M0 collector validates c.Layout ownership, pack/align literal shape, opaque-by-value use, and impossible by-value cycles only. E3.T01 owns actual struct/value representation, native layout evidence, address-taking/copy semantics, and capability-dependent rejection.
Implemented a bounded TypedExpr -> HxcIR -> structural C path for closed anonymous value records. Canonical UTF-8 structural shapes deduplicate aliases and nested records into dependency-first private C structs; construction retains source evaluation order, and copies, projections, field places, addresses, and dereferences remain explicit. Added structural offsetof/align/size assertions, strict C11 and independent C++17 layout consumers at O0/O2, runtime-free production evidence, exact HXC1001 negative boundaries, and metal packed-layout HXC5002 rejection. Compatibility: portable and metal emit the same private record C; auto/none/minimal runtime policies retain zero hxrt for this slice; the existing executable carrier remains hosted; no public ABI stability or general record/class/Dynamic/mutation/equality support is claimed. Evidence: docs/aggregate-lowering.md, test/aggregate_lowering/expected/, docs/specs/bootstrap-inventory.json. Verification: npm run test:aggregate-lowering; npm run format:haxe:check; npm test; git diff --check.
close_reason: E3.T01 acceptance complete: deterministic deduplicated field/layout policy, structural alias compatibility, explicit copy/address HxcIR, metal packed-layout rejection, runtime-free generated C, C/C++17 native layout evidence, documentation/capability updates, and full npm test pass.

title: Stabilize HxcIR source anchors across Haxe server cache reuse
description: The span-lowering performance work found that the pinned Haxe compiler can expose different TypedExpr positions before and after its process-local module cache is populated. In a repeated portable/release request, otherwise identical HxcIR changed an entry block from SpanFixture.hx:47:72-49:3 to 48:3-48:31, and one parenthesized binary expression shifted by one column. Semantics and generated C remained unchanged, but exact HxcIR source evidence was not byte-stable. The span report matrix therefore remains cold; do not normalize this difference away as a test-speed shortcut.
design: Reproduce the first-request/cached-request TypedExpr shapes with the pinned compiler and locate the smallest target-owned stable anchor. Decide whether declaration positions, a typed frontend source-fact record, or another compiler-supported position should own function/body anchors. Preserve precise instruction positions where they are stable. Do not blanket-strip source spans, parse source text heuristically, or treat semantically different source locations as equal. Keep request-local state and malformed/absolute-path validation intact.
acceptance_criteria: A focused warm-server test proves exact HxcIR and diagnostic/source-anchor stability across first, repeated, reordered, and profile/build-changing requests; the fix covers one-expression blocks and parenthesized binary expressions; cold HxcIR snapshots remain unchanged unless an intentional reviewed source-anchor correction is documented; no blanket source-position normalization is introduced; HxcIR, typed-AST, all-source, span, snapshot-owner, and compiler-server gates pass.

title: Make repository writing plain-language and educational
description: The user wants the repository to be a pleasant learning experience. Generalize the existing beginner-readable commit-message rule so source comments, diagnostics, examples, and documentation prefer plain language, define unavoidable technical terms, lead with purpose and observable behavior, and link to deeper contracts without losing precision. Apply the rule immediately to the span-lowering performance explanation that exposed the need.
design: Add a repository-wide AGENTS.md writing contract. Keep normative terminology exact, but introduce it after a short plain-language definition and concrete example. Improve docs/span-lowering.md and docs/test-performance.md so readers understand fixed arrays, spans, borrowing, lowering, runtime-free output, and the distinction between feature compile cost and exhaustive test-harness cost. Preserve deep implementation and evidence sections for expert reference.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md contains a generalized readable/educational writing directive covering code, comments, diagnostics, examples, and docs; the span feature and performance docs define their core terms in plain language and link to deeper detail; focused ownership/policy checks pass; prose does not weaken bounded capability or safety claims.
notes: Implemented the generalized readable/educational writing contract in AGENTS.md and applied it to docs/span-lowering.md plus docs/test-performance.md. The guide now defines fixed array, span, borrowed, lowering, HxcIR, and runtime-free before the detailed contract, and explicitly distinguishes exhaustive test-suite wall time from ordinary feature compile time. Verification passed through capability, typed-boundary, governance, Beads-plan, fixture/snapshot ownership, diagnostics, security, full span semantics/native, full Caxecraft differential/O2, license, and staged guards.
close_reason: Repository-wide plain-language teaching guidance is durable in AGENTS.md; the span and test-performance docs apply it without weakening exact safety/support boundaries; all relevant policy, span, Caxecraft, native, snapshot-owner, and security gates passed.

title: Document the Caxecraft compile-time C seam and cross-target adapter plan
description: Explain every current #if c site at the source and user/architecture documentation levels. Record how the verified custom target supplies the define, that Haxe removes inactive branches at compile time, what the C and non-C carriers do, which behavior remains shared, and why Eval evidence is not a shipped portability claim. Define the long-term adapter and differential-testing plan for Rust/JavaScript experiments without imposing a universal IR or renderer.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every current Caxecraft #if c seam has an accurate inline explanation of its representation or test-plumbing role.
- The example README explains activation, compile-time elimination, supported invocation, current conditional inventory, and user-facing portability limits.
- The domain design inventories every site and gives detailed staged storage/input/render/resource adapter guidance, extraction criteria, and guardrails against scattered target branches.
- Haxe formatting, fixture policy, the Caxecraft C/Eval differential suite, and the centrally managed Caxecraft snapshots pass.
close_reason: Documented every current compile-time seam inline and in the README/domain design; recorded exact activation semantics, limitations, long-term adapter plan, and guardrails. Haxe format, fixture policy, C/Eval domain QA, and central snapshots pass.

title: Centralize exhaustive HxcIR traversal and enforce a validated freeze boundary
description: The Float32 reachability work made a broader architectural risk concrete: whole-program HxcIR traversal is duplicated across runtime-intent analysis, primitive-helper selection, abort selection, use analysis, dependency/header selection, dumping, validation, and emission. Several focused scans intentionally use catch-all cases, so a future child-bearing IR variant can be valid and emitted while remaining invisible to an unrelated dependency or ABI analysis. Separately, CBodyLowering validates an HxcIRProgram but downstream APIs accept the same raw type; RuntimeRequirementAnalyzer states that it requires validated HxcIR while checking only the schema number. Add the smallest output-inert infrastructure that makes traversal coverage and the validated/frozen phase boundary executable before the next substantial HxcIR family is admitted.
design: Add one authoritative deterministic HxcIR child/walk/fold schema with exhaustive closed-variant handling and explicit policies for structural children versus semantic references. Migrate generic whole-program analyses incrementally without changing output. Have HxcIRValidator produce a typed validated wrapper that downstream runtime/helper/CAST-lowering consumers require; raw programs remain available only to builders, malformed fixtures, and validation tests. Treat the validated program as frozen/read-only after construction. Document the node-admission checklist and linear phase boundary. Do not introduce a general pass manager, analysis cache, CAST transform framework, new semantic operation, or universal Reflaxe IR.
acceptance_criteria: An authoritative deterministic traversal covers every HxcIR program/module/type/type-reference/function/block/instruction/place/terminator/failure/cleanup child with no catch-all that can silently classify a child-bearing constructor as a leaf; constructor/sentinel coverage fails when traversal decisions are missing; runtime-intent, helper, abort, and cross-program type/dependency scans use the shared traversal or an explicitly documented exhaustive scoped variant; HxcIRValidator is the only production constructor of a typed validated/frozen program and downstream analysis/emission APIs reject raw HxcIR at compile time; malformed fixtures still exercise raw validation; generated artifacts and registered snapshots remain byte-identical; cold/warm/order determinism, all-source typing, HxcIR negatives, runtime planning, direct imports, native smoke, and full governance pass; docs record phases, ownership, admission questions, and why pass-manager/cache/CAST traversal work remains deferred.

title: Preserve conditional-expression results across HxcIR join blocks
description: The Caxecraft domain exposed a general valid-Haxe lowering defect. A primitive conditional expression consumed directly as a closed-record field or direct-call argument can lower its result in a conditional branch/join, then use that value from a later instruction without a block parameter or stable local load. HxcIRValidator correctly rejects the program with HXC9000 (value is not available at this instruction). Reproduced in PlayerPhysics.step when ternary Float values were record fields and in CaxecraftTrace when a Bool-to-Int ternary was passed directly to mix(). The example currently uses explicit locals/statement assignments, which are semantically equivalent but should not be required.
design: Minimize both shapes independently. Fix conditional-expression lowering so the join defines a value available to its continuation, preferably through the existing result local/load or an explicit block parameter rather than relaxing validation. Preserve left-to-right evaluation and do not reconstruct the value in C. Keep aggregate construction and call argument lowering consumers oblivious to the CFG implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Add minimized positive fixtures for (1) a primitive ternary used directly in a closed-record constructor field and (2) a primitive ternary used directly as a direct-call argument. Both must validate through TypedExpr -> schema-9 HxcIR -> structural C, run under strict C11 at O0/O2, and match Eval exactly. HxcIR must show a valid join/result handoff with no cross-block temporary use, reversed input and repeated/compiler-server renders must be deterministic, and existing evaluation-order/aggregate/function/Caxecraft gates must pass. Add a focused negative validator fixture proving that genuinely unavailable cross-block values remain HXC9000.

title: Design and implement an ergonomic hxc dev/watch loop
description: Give reflaxe.c projects one obvious development command that watches the complete declared build-input graph, reuses a safely owned Haxe compiler server, incrementally regenerates and compiles C, and optionally restarts the built program. The design should learn from reflaxe.elixir's HaxeBuildInputs, HaxeWatcher, HaxeServer, mix haxe.watch, status/error reporting, and successful-output promotion, while accounting for C's additional codegen, native compile/link, runtime-slice, header, and process-restart stages.
design: Add an hxc dev or hxc watch surface only after the normal hxc configuration, command router, native build plan, and build command exist; it must be a thin orchestration mode over those same typed models rather than a parallel build system. One-shot hxc build remains direct and deterministic. A long-running dev command owns any haxe --wait process explicitly, keys reuse by project root plus exact toolchain/config/target/profile identity, never attaches to an unrelated external server by default, records a recoverable non-secret cookie, and cleans up children on signals. Discover watched inputs from the effective typed configuration and recursive HXML/native dependency graph, support explicit extra inputs for macro reads, debounce/coalesce platform events, and provide a bounded polling fallback. Preserve the last known-good executable/library while a rebuild fails; publish generated/native artifacts only after a successful phase using existing ownership and atomic replacement contracts. Optional run mode should forward arguments safely, restart only after successful link, and terminate the prior child with a bounded graceful-to-force policy. Human and versioned JSON event modes should expose invalidation reasons, phases, timing, diagnostics, server ownership, and child lifecycle without stdout corruption. Benchmark cold, warm, no-op, single-Haxe-edit, header-edit, and relink cycles. Do not promise source-level hot code reload; this is fast rebuild plus optional process restart.
acceptance_criteria: A documented hxc dev/watch command builds once, watches a canonical inspectable input graph, and rebuilds the minimal safe Haxe/codegen/native stages on create/change/delete/rename events across supported hosts. One-shot build does not spawn or leak a compiler server; watch mode owns, reuses, validates, relocates, and reliably stops its server and optional child process, including stale-cookie and occupied-port cases. Failed rebuilds emit source-positioned diagnostics and retain the last good published artifacts; successful rebuilds atomically publish outputs and optionally restart the program with safely forwarded arguments and exit reporting. Human and JSON event streams are deterministic and tested. Recursive HXML, classpath, config, macro extra-input, generated ownership, native source/header, runtime-plan, toolchain, and removed-input invalidation have executable tests. Watcher event storms are debounced without dropping a final change, symlink/path escapes fail closed, and Ctrl+C/deadline tests leave no orphan processes. A reproducible benchmark records cold/warm/no-op/edit-to-ready latency and guards a reviewed p95 budget. The hello and Caxecraft docs demonstrate the same command without bespoke watcher scripts.
notes: Observed performance signal from generated-C readability work on 2026-07-19: a cold Caxecraft C render with the large HxcIR/static-initialization report enabled took about 47 seconds on a busy development host, while the earlier quick end-to-end run took about 127.7 seconds. These are diagnostic observations, not portable baselines. Before choosing a different implementation language or host, add request-local phase timings for Haxe typing, source-fact capture, lowering subphases (including span lowering), validation, naming/layout, report serialization, output writing, and native compile/run. Keep heavy dumps opt-in; distinguish cold, warm, no-op, one-module edit, generated-C-only native, and full CI paths; cache only immutable facts with explicit invalidation; batch and parallelize independent native lanes. Set reviewed p95 budgets from reproducible idle-host measurements. Eval remains the differential oracle even if a future native hxc host accelerates orchestration or selected analyses.

title: Build a sanitized cross-target semantics research bundle and GPT-5.6 prompt
description: After the active compiler and toolchain priorities are resolved, prepare a self-contained research package for a GPT-5.6 Pro architecture study. The study must examine whether Haxe source, TypedExpr, portable APIs, capability contracts, standard-library parity, and differential tests are sufficient for applications shared across the C, Rust, Go, Elixir, Ruby, JavaScript/TypeScript, and OCaml target family; it must separately identify evidence that would justify any shared semantic analysis or IR. Curate committed source snapshots from reflaxe.c, haxe.rust, reflaxe.go, haxe.elixir.codex, haxe.ruby, genes, haxe.ocaml/hxhx where relevant, and the pinned upstream Haxe compiler source. Do not copy Git history, Beads databases, dependencies, build products, local paths, credentials, or unrelated untracked files.
design: Treat docs/hxc-ir.md and docs/research/repo-patterns.md as the current evidence-based hypothesis: TypedExpr is already the common frontend IR, portable observable contracts should be shared first, and target-specific semantic plans/ASTs preserve target strengths. The prompt must invite falsification and compare strings, numbers, null, exceptions, collections, dynamic/reflection, concurrency, I/O, ownership, ABI, runtime selection, target capabilities, macros, and standard-library ownership. Build the archive from explicit manifests or committed-tree exports, record repository revisions and omissions, scan the staged tree and final archive for secrets and absolute paths, and verify archive contents and extraction.
acceptance_criteria: A detailed GPT-5.6 Pro prompt asks concrete architectural questions, supplies repository context, distinguishes portable contracts from compiler representations, and requests phased experiments plus decision criteria. A machine-readable manifest records every included repository, revision, selected paths, licenses, exclusions, dirty-state handling, and checksums. The ZIP contains only curated committed sources and the prompt/manifest, excludes .git, .beads, node_modules, caches, generated outputs, credentials, absolute local paths, and unrelated untracked files, and passes secret/path scans plus extraction verification. The prompt explicitly evaluates when a shared analysis or IR would help, when it would be counterproductive, how target-native strengths remain available, and which stdlib/capability/differential-test infrastructure is required. The bundle is produced only after higher-priority compiler/toolchain work and its local path and size are reported without claiming the archive is a versioned release artifact.
close_reason: Delivered the reproducible GPT-5.6 Pro cross-target semantics prompt and sanitized 14-repository committed-source bundle. The 29,156,439-byte ZIP passed pinned Gitleaks scans, absolute-path and secret heuristics, CRC and safe-extraction checks, forbidden-path review, and a byte-for-byte rebuild; its SHA-256 is 3d3da78fa84a7ad15be68dc5c4c9d292fece4d507df6cfd694ff53205e5c3e95. The archive is explicitly a local research artifact, not a release.

title: Document TypedExpr and HxcIR architecture boundary
description: Explain why Haxe TypedExpr is already the shared high-level IR, when direct TypedExpr-to-target-AST lowering is sufficient, and why haxe.c retains a narrower semantic HxcIR for C-sensitive sequencing, places, failure, cleanup, lifetime, bounds, representation, and runtime-intent decisions. Record sibling Go, Rust, Elixir, and OCaml target architectures and define evidence-first criteria for any future shared Reflaxe semantic layer. Clarify that changing the compiler implementation language to OCaml would improve representation ergonomics but would not remove the Haxe-to-C semantic gap.
design: Keep HxcIR target-owned and deliberately smaller than TypedExpr. Do not mirror the Haxe AST or extract a universal Reflaxe IR. New HxcIR forms must name a semantic invariant that cannot remain safely implicit until CAST emission. Treat sibling repository observations as descriptive evidence, not normative dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: docs/hxc-ir.md contains the detailed rationale, alternatives, layer ownership, sibling comparison, implementation-language analysis, and future shared-layer admission criteria; docs/architecture.md gives the concise operative rule and links to the detailed contract; docs/research/repo-patterns.md records the inspected sibling pipelines accurately; documentation links and repository policy checks pass without changing capability claims.
close_reason: Documented TypedExpr/HxcIR ownership, direct-lowering alternative, sibling architectures, universal-IR failure modes, evidence-first shared-layer criteria, and implementation-language implications. Verified: npm run test:hxc-ir; npm run test:governance-policy; git diff --check.

title: Polish Caxecraft as a public showcase and compiler QA example
description:
Present Caxecraft as a compelling but evidence-bounded showcase once gameplay, performance, and e2e gates are real.

Create a concise build/run/controls guide, architecture walkthrough, RaylibHx raw-versus-semantic comparison, generated-C inspection guide, reproducible performance report, platform support table, troubleshooting, and an automated fresh-checkout smoke. Add deterministic screenshots or short media only from the tested build and retain enough source-to-generated-C examples for readers to see the direct raylib calls and runtime plan.

The narrative should emphasize Haxe ergonomics, native C integration, transparent escape hatches, and compiler QA without calling the compiler generally complete, production-ready, portable, or faster than C.

Non-scope: a release announcement before gates pass, hand-edited generated artifacts, benchmark cherry-picking, or branding the future Rust/JavaScript experiment as delivered.
acceptance_criteria:
- A new user can provision, build, run, understand controls, inspect generated C, and reproduce the declared benchmark from the documented path.
- Media and metrics identify the exact source commit, raylib lock, platform/toolchain, and configuration that produced them.
- The walkthrough contrasts representative raw and semantic calls and shows their generated direct C shape and exact runtime/build plans.
- Capability/product claims match the machine-readable manifest and every platform/performance statement links executable evidence.
- Fresh-checkout documentation tests and the complete example gate pass without ../raylib, developer-machine paths, or unstated global setup.

title: Prove RaylibHx packed-consumer and extraction readiness
description:
Prove that the incubated companion can become an independent raylibhx-style Haxelib without forcing a premature repository split.

Stage an exact package artifact containing public raw/semantic source, generated inventories/locks as appropriate, license and provenance notices, documentation, target/toolchain compatibility metadata, and tests. Install it into an isolated Haxelib repository and build Caxecraft as a consumer with no source-repository shortcut.

Resolve the eventual package name and compatibility posture against the existing hxcpp-specific raylib-hx project through the architecture decision. Extraction must preserve the C target as the current supported backend while leaving public APIs free of private compiler implementation imports.

Non-scope: publishing to Haxelib, moving Git history, supporting hxcpp/Rust/JavaScript automatically, bundling raylib binaries, or closing incubation before the evidence passes.
acceptance_criteria:
- An isolated pack/install/consume test builds and runs Caxecraft without private repo classpaths, sibling paths, unpublished generated files, or ambient global dependencies.
- The staged artifact has deterministic contents, appropriate raylib and companion license/provenance notices, version/support policy, ownership documentation, and precise capability/omission tables.
- Public package source imports only stable public Haxe/c.* contracts; private compiler modules and test seams are absent.
- Raw and semantic APIs, generated-source ownership, upgrade/regeneration workflow, rollback, and post-extraction maintenance boundaries are documented.
- The extraction checklist has objective pass/fail gates and identifies any remaining blockers; completing this task authorizes a later split but does not perform or publish it.

title: Expand RaylibHx to a complete precise-or-omitted raylib 6.0 inventory
description:
Grow the coherent Caxecraft core into an extraction-quality view of the complete public raylib 6.0 header after the generic E6 bindgen and lock machinery exists.

Every public type, alias, field, enum, constant, function, callback, variadic surface, pointer, resource, and platform-conditioned declaration must appear exactly once in a deterministic inventory. Representable raw declarations are emitted mechanically from pinned Clang facts. Unsupported or unsafe declarations remain explicit omissions with a reason, diagnostic policy, required compiler capability, and owner. Curated semantic wrappers remain separately owned.

The coverage model must distinguish declaration presence, ABI verification, compile evidence, runtime exercise, ownership review, and semantic-wrapper support. A narrow passing fixture must never promote a neighboring API.

Non-scope: silently binding private raylib internals, treating rlparser JSON as ABI authority, auto-generating unreviewed semantic wrappers, promising every platform configuration, or publishing the package.
acceptance_criteria:
- The complete raylib 6.0 public header has a deterministic one-to-one inventory with no catch-all and no unclassified declaration.
- Supported raw externs regenerate byte-identically from the exact lock and compile against the pinned headers; unsupported forms fail closed with named owners.
- ABI probes and drift reports cover all admitted layouts/constants/signatures and clearly distinguish source additions, removals, changes, and target-conditioned differences.
- Pointer depth, constness, nullability, retained/borrowed lifetime, ownership, callbacks, variadics, thread affinity, and failure behavior are machine-readable and reviewed.
- Representative tests cover every declaration family, while Caxecraft continues to consume only its minimal coherent subset.
- A clean regeneration changes only centrally owned generated artifacts and never embeds checkout, temp, locale, timestamp, or discovery-order facts.

title: Add chunked rendering and measured Caxecraft performance
description:
Optimize Caxecraft only after preserving a measured playable baseline. Introduce a small chunk grid, dirty-chunk invalidation after edits, and visible-face extraction, then select the narrowest raylib rendering technique justified by evidence.

Candidate techniques include instanced cube drawing or generated chunk meshes. The choice must account for FFI breadth, ownership/lifetime, rebuild cost, memory, draw calls, GPU/driver behavior, and generated-code inspectability. Resource-owning APIs require explicit typed ownership and cleanup on every exit edge; they must not be smuggled into the first raw-core binding.

Keep deterministic terrain/gameplay unchanged. Retain the baseline renderer as a regression/oracle configuration where practical.

Non-scope: a general renderer, ECS, occlusion engine, GPU-specific shader architecture, arbitrary-distance streaming, aesthetic effects, or an unmeasured fast claim.
acceptance_criteria:
- Before/after benchmarks record scene, seed, viewport, raylib build, compiler/target, warmup, sample method, frame/update time, draw calls, rebuild time, memory/allocation counts, generated C size, and native binary size.
- Dirty chunks rebuild only when affected; hidden faces are omitted; edits update neighboring chunk boundaries correctly.
- The steady-state loop performs no heap allocation and selects no new hxrt feature unless a separately accepted semantic requirement proves it unavoidable.
- The selected batching path has explicit ownership, deterministic cleanup, failure handling, and sanitizer coverage.
- Results are compared with a small equivalent hand-written C/raylib baseline under the repository benchmark policy; differences and limitations are reported honestly.
- No performance, zero-cost, or optimized claim is added to capability/product documentation unless the reproducible thresholds and regression gates pass.

title: Add a typed native TUI frontend to the todo showcase
description:
Context: The canonical E9 todo showcase currently proves a scriptable CLI, SQLite integration, compile-time schema/command generation, and portable domain logic, but it does not prove an interactive native terminal application. A TUI is a strong product demonstration because it combines Haxe ergonomics with ordinary C libraries and native executable behavior.

Scope: Add a second todo frontend over the exact same typed domain, validation, command model, and SQLite repository used by the CLI. Exercise a typed terminal-library binding or target-owned terminal adapter, input events, resize, incremental rendering, Unicode text, resource ownership, errors, and deterministic cleanup. Keep the CLI as the non-interactive baseline and acceptance interface.

Non-scope: A graphical desktop UI, compiler special cases, raw __c__ injection, duplicated business logic, or a claim that every terminal/platform is supported. Split any new compiler/runtime/library capability into its owning bead rather than hiding it in example code.

Product value: The example should make visible why Haxe helps: exhaustive typed UI states, reusable portable domain code, compile-time schema/command derivation, and safer native-library integration while still producing inspectable warning-clean C.
design: Use four explicit layers: portable todo domain; SQLite repository; scriptable CLI adapter; interactive TUI adapter. The CLI and TUI must share application commands and services rather than call each other or fork the domain. Select the terminal backend through the normal bindgen/adapter policy after portability and ABI evidence; do not bake a third-party ABI into handwritten Haxe declarations without probes. CI should drive a deterministic terminal transcript or pseudo-terminal harness rather than relying on timing or screenshots alone.
acceptance_criteria:
- examples/todo-tui has a schema-valid case.json with role example and declared semantic assertions.
- CLI and TUI share the same domain, validation, application commands, migrations, and SQLite repository; no business-logic fork exists.
- The TUI covers list/filter/add/edit/complete/delete/help, terminal resize, empty/error states, and clean shutdown.
- Terminal integration uses typed bindings or a target-owned adapter, contains no raw __c__, and documents ownership, encoding, portability, and runtime effects.
- A deterministic transcript or pseudo-terminal harness runs in CI without depending on wall-clock timing; generated C is warning-clean under required GCC and Clang lanes.
- Runtime-plan, generated-C inspection, sanitizer/resource-cleanup evidence, and supported/unsupported hosted environments are documented honestly.
- The walkthrough explains the Haxe advantage and retains the CLI as a stable automation/accessibility path.

title: Reconcile absent supplied Repomix evidence snapshots
description:
Context: AGENTS.md and PRD §2/§32/§41 describe reference/repomix/*.xml snapshots as the primary local sibling-target, Reflaxe, Haxe, and hxcpp precedent, but this checkout has no reference/ directory. E1.T06 attempted the required sibling-pattern review and could only use the pinned local Reflaxe source plus docs/research/repo-patterns.md.

Scope: Restore the checksum-reviewed evidence snapshots or correct the normative inventory/research claims and provide a reproducible acquisition/verification path. Keep provenance and licensing explicit.

Non-scope: Copying sibling compiler implementation into reflaxe.c or widening E1.T06.

Validation: Documented snapshot paths exist and hashes/provenance validate, or all present-tense claims are corrected and automated structure checks prevent recurrence.

Compatibility: Research/governance assets only; no profile, runtime, environment, generated-C, or public-ABI change.
acceptance_criteria: The documented evidence paths either exist with verified hashes/provenance and searchable contents, or the PRD/research inventory explicitly marks them absent and points to a reproducible source; CI detects future drift.

title: Reconcile absent documented example seeds
description:
Context: PRD §19, §23, and §32.2 describe examples/hello, no-runtime, pointlib, shared-library, cpp-shim, and todo-cli as seeded assets, but this checkout has no examples/ directory. The fixture-policy audit in haxe_c-20x exposed the mismatch.

Scope: Decide whether the seeds should be restored, or correct the inventory and related README/PRD claims to distinguish planned examples from present native fixtures. Keep machine-readable bootstrap inventory parity aligned once haxe_c-xk3 supplies it.

Non-scope: Implementing compiler lowering or making the planned examples executable. Their existing milestone tasks remain authoritative.

Validation: rg --files examples agrees with the accepted inventory; documentation/capability checks reject future present-versus-planned drift.

Compatibility: Documentation/scaffold inventory only; no profile, runtime, environment, generated-C, or public-ABI change.

Documentation: Update PRD §19/§23/§32, README capability language, and the machine inventory as applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Either the documented example seed directories and honest non-functional markers are restored, or all present-tense inventory claims are corrected to planned/absent; an automated inventory check prevents recurrence; no example is counted as a test without a declared assertion contract.

title: Reconcile missing machine-readable bootstrap inventory
description: PRD §32.2 says the Markdown inventory is also available as docs/specs/bootstrap-inventory.json for automation, but this checkout contains neither that file nor a schema/validator. Reconcile the normative claim without widening E1.T02: add a schema-validated, drift-checked inventory or adopt an explicit decision that corrects and redirects the PRD claim.
acceptance_criteria: A canonical machine-readable inventory exists at the documented path and CI verifies its schema plus component/status/gap parity with PRD §32.2; alternatively, an accepted architecture/documentation decision removes or redirects the claim and link checks pass.

title: [HXC-E8-T08] Implement source maps and native diagnostic remapping
description:
Stable plan key: E8.T08
Requirement IDs: HXC-COMP-013, HXC-COMP-014, HXC-CLI-008
PRD sections: §20.2

Context: Map C compiler/linker errors and optional debug locations back to Haxe source and generated artifacts.

Scope: Map C compiler/linker errors and optional debug locations back to Haxe source and generated artifacts.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E1.T02, E2.T02, E8.T04

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- #line mode and sidecar map mode are supported/compared.
- Generated helper errors identify originating Haxe feature where possible.
- Raw native diagnostics remain available.
- Paths are normalized and do not break reproducibility.

title: [HXC-E7-T09] Generate optional header-only C++ RAII wrappers
description:
Stable plan key: E7.T09
Requirement IDs: HXC-PROD-007, HXC-ABI-003, HXC-ABI-005
PRD sections: §16.4

Context: Provide opt-in C++ convenience types over the stable C ABI without making C++ ABI part of the product contract.

Scope: Provide opt-in C++ convenience types over the stable C ABI without making C++ ABI part of the product contract.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Stable plan key: E9.T07
Requirement IDs: HXC-FFI-009, HXC-MAC-008, HXC-EX-006
PRD sections: §19.2

Context: Use generated SQLite bindings/codecs/statements with explicit errors, resource ownership, transactions, and migrations.

Scope: Use generated SQLite bindings/codecs/statements with explicit errors, resource ownership, transactions, and migrations.

Non-scope: Work outside this outcome and its acceptance criteria. Create a linked discovered-from issue instead of silently widening scope.

Dependencies: E9.T06, E6.T12, E5.T09

Validation: Satisfy every acceptance item with executable evidence and the applicable PRD §40 definition-of-done gates; record exact commands and artifacts in the close reason.

Compatibility: Assess and document portable/metal, runtime-policy, environment, generated-C, and public-ABI impact where applicable. Do not harden an unresolved public contract without an accepted ADR.

Documentation: Update affected ADRs, documentation, diagnostics, capability data, and ledgers required by the acceptance criteria and PRD §40.
acceptance_criteria:
- CRUD, migration, rollback, busy/error, and corrupt-input tests pass.
- Statements/database close on every path.
- SQL injection is impossible through value parameters.
- Generated/native allocation behavior is inspectable.

title: [HXC-E9] Examples, todo CLI, documentation, and adoption
description:
Stable plan key: E9
Milestone: M9
Source: docs/specs/beads-plan.json and docs/PRD.md

Outcome: Prove the product through honest, polished examples and a macro-powered SQLite todo CLI.

Scope: Coordinate the acceptance-tested child issues for this milestone. Parent-child hierarchy is structural and does not imply blocking.

Execution: Children may proceed in parallel unless an explicit blocking edge says otherwise.

## Success Criteria

- examples use no unapproved `__c__`;
- todo generated C is reviewed against a hand-written baseline;
- docs state limitations honestly;
- benchmark results are reproducible.

title: Explore Rust and JavaScript adapters for the Caxecraft domain after the C showcase
description:
Follow up only after the native C/raylib showcase is playable and evidence-backed. Reuse the renderer-independent domain seam to test whether the same game logic can compile through future Rust and JavaScript targets while each target keeps an idiomatic platform/rendering adapter.

This issue is intentionally outside the RaylibHx/Caxecraft delivery epic so it cannot delay or distort the C-first design. Evaluate the real cost of replacing fixed c.CArray storage, c.Float32 boundary conversions, and raylib input/rendering with portable or target-specific carriers. Use shared deterministic traces to separate domain portability from rendering/backend differences.

Non-scope: imposing lowest-common-denominator APIs on the first C version, promising source compatibility before experiments, or treating raylib as a Rust/JavaScript abstraction.
acceptance_criteria:
- The accepted C domain boundary and deterministic trace suite are inventoried before any porting change.
- Rust and JavaScript prototypes isolate storage, clock/input, rendering, and native-resource adapters while keeping gameplay algorithms shared where semantics truly match.
- Cross-target state traces report integer, floating-point, iteration, and collision differences explicitly.
- Performance, code size, runtime, ownership, and ergonomics tradeoffs are measured per target.
- Any common abstraction is introduced only after two working adapters prove it, with no regression to the direct C/raylib path.

title: Evaluate native OCaml promotion for the haxe.c target core
description: After reproducible phase profiling and after the haxe.ocaml M22 shared-host SDK is executable rather than planning-only, evaluate compiling haxe.c target logic from the same Haxe sources into a native OCaml Reflaxe plugin or builtin. The goal is lower compiler latency without a semantic fork or loss of the direct stock-Haxe recovery path.
design: Keep the stock-Haxe/Reflaxe activation and TypedExpr capture as a thin host adapter. Make the target core request-local and host-neutral where evidence justifies it: frozen typed facts/HxcIR, validators, optimization and representation plans, naming, declaration/layout planning, CAST, printing, manifests, and transactional artifact planning remain one Haxe implementation. Follow ../haxe.ocaml M22: one semantic target core, preferably one promoted payload, with only typed/versioned host adapters and negotiated services. Do not introduce a second C backend, a universal IR, per-expression RPC, or speculative abstraction solely for this future lane. Profile first; native promotion is accepted only if it improves a named dominant cost after ordinary algorithmic, caching, reporting, and process-reuse work.
acceptance_criteria: A source inventory separates macro/compiler-object dependencies from promotable target-core code and names the smallest adapter boundary; the exact haxe.ocaml/hxhx SDK version and capability contract are pinned; the same frozen program produces byte-identical normal C/build/ABI artifacts and equivalent structured diagnostics through evaluated and native routes; cold, warm, no-op, one-module edit, peak-RSS, and backend-only benchmarks show a reviewed material improvement and attribute remaining Haxe typing cost; compiler-server/reentrancy, cancellation, malformed transport, ABI mismatch, plugin load/unload, and fallback tests pass; direct pinned Haxe/HXML compilation remains documented and functional; no readiness claim is made while the shared stock-Haxe/hxhx plugin ABI remains planning-only.
notes:
This records the project-owner hypothesis from 2026-07-19. ../haxe.ocaml/docs/00-project/REFLAXE_NATIVE_COMPILER_SDK_M22_PLAN.md explicitly aims for normal Haxe + portable Reflaxe, one target implementation, native OCaml promotion, and the same payload in stock Haxe or hxhx. Expected reuse is nearly all semantic target logic, not literally all host glue. It may accelerate backend execution; it cannot by itself remove Haxe parse/type time.
The intended high-upside experiment is the combined route, not only a native backend under stock Haxe: a native hxhx parse/type/macro host feeding the same haxe.c core compiled by reflaxe.ocaml as a plugin or builtin could remove Eval from both major compiler-side execution layers. Current hard prerequisites include parity for this repositorys pinned Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 TypedExpr/macro/custom-target surface (hxhx currently declares Haxe 4.3.7 compatibility as its active goal) and a shipped M22 shared host SDK. Compare end-to-end and phase-isolated timings; do not infer speed from native packaging alone.
Project-owner clarification: this is deliberately optional and may never be pursued. It must not block the normal Haxe/Reflaxe compiler, Caxecraft, release milestones, or near-term performance work. Revisit only if profiling shows remaining compiler-host overhead worth the added SDK/plugin complexity.
